channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Harris says she's ready if Trump tries to prematurely declare victory, isn't worried about sexism
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris said Tuesday that her team is prepared to challenge Donald Trump if he tries to prematurely declare victory in the 2024 election — but she's first focused on beating the Republican nominee.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Border arrests fall in September in last monthly gauge before US elections
item.description: U.S. authorities say arrests for illegally crossing the border from Mexico fell 7% in September to a more than four-year low.
item.dc:creator: Elliot Spagat, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden calls for Trump to be 'politically' locked up at New Hampshire event
item.description: On Tuesday, President Biden called for former President Trump to be politically "locked up" over accusations that he is a threat to democracy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lloyd Austin warns against isolationism and insists Ukraine absolutely can win war against Russia
item.description: Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin defended U.S. restrictions on long-range missiles in Ukraine and insisted the war-ravaged nation ‘absolutely’ can defeat Russia even without such capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia and Iran may fuel violent post-election protests in the US, intelligence officials warn
item.description: Intelligence officials warn that Russia and Iran may encourage violent protests in the United States after next month's election.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: McConnell, GOP descend on Nebraska to save Deb Fischer's endangered Senate seat
item.description: Republicans are quickly getting involved in the Nebraska Senate race to save Sen. Deb Fischer from an upset against opponent Dan Osborn.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris claims Trump win would be 'catastrophic' for Social Security, but first term tells different tale
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris slammed former President Trump’s plan to eliminate income taxes on Social Security benefits, citing a report that it would worsen the program's finances.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris will campaign in Texas to highlight state's abortion ban in a pitch to battleground voters
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris will head to reliably Republican Texas just 10 days before Election Day in an effort to refocus her campaign against former President Donald Trump on reproductive care, which Democrats see as a make-or-break issue this year.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AP Decision Notes: What to expect in North Carolina on Election Day
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — North Carolina voters will cast ballots for president and governor in the Nov. 5 general election while many in the state are still dealing with the aftermath and massive destruction caused by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT YOON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dolton mayor Tiffany Henyard dubbed 'America's worst mayor' running for reelection
item.description: The mayor of Dolton, Ill., Tiffany Henyard, is likely the most controversial mayor ever elected in the town of 20,000, facing multiple controversies.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Early voting kicks off in battleground Wisconsin with a push from Obama and Walz
item.description: In-person early voting has kicked off across battleground Wisconsin, with former President Barack Obama and Democratic vice presidential nominee Tim Walz hosting a rally in liberal Madison.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump selling 'MAGADonald's' shirts after drive-thru appearance
item.description: After Trump’s visit to the Bucks County franchise, McDonald’s sought to emphasize its political neutrality and said it does not endorse political candidates.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris bets her policies can attract Latino voters while Trump touts his time as president to them
item.description: Both Kamala Harris and Donald Trump are highlighting their economic policies in their outreach to Latino voters with Election Day just two weeks away.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Politicians to swarm Las Vegas as Election Day nears
item.description: Las Vegas is set for a busy week of Presidential politics as the moments tick away until Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court turns away challenge to consumer safety agency’s structure
item.description: The Supreme Court announced Monday it won’t take up a case that could have delivered another blow to federal agencies. Two conservative groups sought to challenge the structure of the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC), which oversees making sure the country’s consumer products are safe.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Congress’s to-do list grows while on the campaign trail
item.description: As Congress enters a critical stretch to the November elections, a lengthy to-do list of critical funding items is piling in its absence.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Harris says Trump is 'cruel' at Georgia rally as she spotlights abortion restrictions
item.description: Kamala Harris has said former President Trump is “cruel” for how he talked about the family of a Georgia mother who died after waiting for treatment for complications from an abortion pill.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump's restless week on the trail ends with a rally riff about Arnold Palmer's manhood
item.description: Former President Donald Trump circuitously referenced golfer Arnold Palmer's genitalia but more directly insulted Vice President Harris at a winding rally in Latrobe, Pa., Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler, MPR News

item.title: Harris and Trump hold competing rallies in Michigan
item.description: While the two candidates have been crisscrossing the swing states for weeks, this is the first time they are literally crossing paths, with each of them holding events in the suburbs north of Detroit.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea, Asma Khalid, and Eric McDaniel, MPR News

item.title: Election 2024: How the 'New York Seven' could decide the House
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York voters have much to consider on Election Day. In addition to voting for the next president of the United States, New Yorkers will be asked to decide on a proposed amendment to the state Constitution, a U.S. Senate seat, and House representatives.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Trump expresses frustration about calls to deploy Haley
item.description: “But let me just tell you: Nikki Haley and I fought, and I beat her by 50, 60, 90 points. I beat her in her own state. I beat Nikki badly.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Almost 23% of people say politics influences where they live: Poll
item.description: "Some go so far as to consider moving to find that alignment," Danielle Hale, chief economist for Realtor.com, said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, Fox 5

item.title: Eric Adams welcomes new administration members amid wave of resignations
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Some new faces are joining Mayor Eric Adams' administration as it works to stabilize City Hall after a series of high-level exits. A new city schools chancellor and a new deputy mayor for public safety are taking the reins.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11
